summary: Iteration 3 testing: validated all three enhancements (WS reconnect UX assets, real audio assets, DB persistence + rematch). Backend full game flow + rematch + DB persistence verified end-to-end. All 7 audio assets serve 200/audio/mpeg. Frontend home loads with audio toggle button working, zero console errors.
passed: Game auto-advances to status=finished after final round (with auto-end on all-answered)
MongoDB games collection: record inserted with code, finished_at, rounds_played, round_duration_s, results[] sorted by score desc; total 2 game docs present
POST /api/rooms/{code}/rematch by non-host returns 403 ('Only host can start a rematch')
After rematch: status='lobby', all scores=0, photos hidden again
POST /api/rooms/{code}/rematch in lobby returns 400 ('Game must be finished first')
All 7 audio files (/audio/music|correct|wrong|tick|click|join|win.mp3) return 200 with content-type audio/mpeg
Frontend home loads (title 'Emergent | Fullstack App'), Create/Join Room buttons render
data-testid=audio-toggle-btn present and clickable; toggling on/off produces ZERO console errors
In-page fetch of /audio/music.mp3 returns 200 audio/mpeg from frontend origin
notes: Reconnect-banner and conn-ok badge UI flows were not exercised in-browser (would require multi-tab/WS simulation); however Room.jsx changes are isolated and backend WS endpoint behavior (broadcast on connect/disconnect) was confirmed indirectly during the full game flow. DB persistence happens via persist_finished_game() which is invoked both via end_round_after_delay and _advance_after_result – this run hit the all-answered path and the record was still persisted correctly.
